Section Title: Multi-Asset Allocation: The 2025 Winner's Strategy for 2026 > ... > Other Tata Group Sites
Content:
**Understand the Difference between Active and Passive Multi-Asset Funds:** Active MAAF funds have the liberty to shift allocations, while passive multi-asset FoFs track specific market indexes. While fund managers actively rebalance portfolios for the former, rebalancing happens systematically for the latter.

More Details
About HDFC Multi-Asset Active FoF - Regular Plan
HDFC Multi-Asset Active FoF - Regular Plan is a hybrid mutual fund scheme of HDFC Mutual Fund. Launched on May 05, 2021, it is currently managed by Anil Bamboli, Srinivasan Ramamurthy and Bhagyesh Kagalkar. The fund has an expense ratio of 1.14% with an overall AUM (Assets Under Management) of ₹5,229 Cr.
The scheme seeke to capital appreciation by managing the asset allocation between equity oriented, debt oriented and gold ETF schemes. The fund allows minimum lumpsum investment of ₹100 and minimum SIP of ₹100.
Investment Strategy
The scheme seeke to capital appreciation by managing the asset allocation between equity oriented, debt oriented and gold ETF schemes.
Suitability
Multi Asset Allocation funds are suitable for investors:
Section Title: HDFC Multi-Asset Active FoF - Regular Plan > ... > Min. No of Cheques
Content:
Looking to invest in a portfolio of equity, debt and commodity for moderate returns
Who prefer to let fund manager decide how much to invest in equity, debt and commodity
Aiming for long term wealth creation
With a 5-8 year investment horizon
Who are new/unfamiliar with market's ups and downs
Note:
Invest only through SIP
Capital Gains Taxation
The following tax treatment is based upon last 12-months asset allocation and may vary from other funds in the category.
If the mutual fund units are sold after 2 years from the date of investment, gains are taxed at the rate of 12.5%.
If the mutual fund units are sold within 2 years from the date of investment, entire amount of gain is added to the investors' income and taxed according to the applicable slab rate.
No tax is to be paid as long as you continue to hold the units.

Fund of Funds
DIRECT  REGULAR
An open ended fund of fund investing in equity oriented, Debt oriented and Gold ETF schemes. The Fund follows a systematic and process driven asset allocation approach based on the financial model.
The Scheme could be considered as an option to meet diversified asset allocation needs of investors across asset classes.
